tool.name: symb_to_attr_tool

param.name: color_encoding
param.dialogReference: Leaflet mode uses 6-digit HEX with a separate opacity field; Custom option allows 8-digit HEX or CSS RGBA formats.

tool.summary:
The first tool, Symbology to Attributes, accepts an feature layer as input and extracts supported primary symbology types, specifically Single Symbol, Unique Values, and Graduated Colors. All other primary symbology types are not supported. In addition, value expressions are not supported for any symbology type, including the supported ones.

Symbology is derived using ArcPy and layer CIM, including default colors for out-of-range values. The tool creates a derived copy of the source feature class associated with the input feature layer and enriches it with additional attribute fields containing the extracted symbology. It also supports configurable color encoding: by default, Leaflet mode uses 6-digit HEX with a separate opacity field, while a custom option allows 8-digit HEX or CSS RGBA formats.

scriptExample.title: Symbology to Attributes
scriptExample.para: # Run the tool from ArcGIS Pro interface
scriptExample.code: # Run the tool from ArcGIS Pro interface

tool.usage: Select an input ArcGIS feature layer containing point, line, or polygon features. Specify an output feature class location. Choose a color encoding mode (Leaflet Format, CSS RGBA, or 8-digit HEX). The tool will extract supported symbology types and store them as attribute fields in the output feature class.

searchKeys.keyword: Symbology; Feature Layer; ArcGIS Pro; Python Toolbox; CIM; ArcPy; Graduated Colors; Unique Values; Single Symbol; Attribute Conversion; GIS; Cartography; Color Encoding
